claims.claim: On 6 May 2026, the MACC's outgoing chief said the agency would seek charges over Malaysia's RM1.1 billion Arm Holdings semiconductor deal.
claims.source: Malay Mail, 6 May 2026: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/05/06/azam-baki-macc-recommends-charges-against-two-in-arm-holdings-chip-probe/218915; The Edge Malaysia, 6 May 2026: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/802481
claims.correction: Use 'MACC recommended charges against two' or 'MACC planned to submit a recommendation to the DPP/AGC', not 'moved to charge', because MACC itself does not decide prosecution.

claims.claim: The Arm Holdings deal was signed in March 2025.
claims.source: MIDA, 5 March 2025: https://www.mida.gov.my/announcement-month/march/; MIDA event page: https://www.mida.gov.my/events/mida-and-arm-limited-forge-agreement-to-boost-malaysias-semiconductor-industry/
claims.correction: The precise official formulation is that MIDA and ARM Limited announced or signed the partnership on 5 March 2025.

claims.claim: The deal value was RM1.1 billion or about US$250 million.
claims.source: Reuters, 5 March 2025: https://www.reuters.com/markets/asia/malaysia-minister-says-pay-arm-holdings-250-million-chips-design-blueprints-2025-03-05/; MIDA news repost, 5 March 2025: https://www.mida.gov.my/mida-news/anwar-rm1-1b-partnership-with-uks-arm-holdings-marks-beginning-of-malaysias-second-semiconductor-wave/
claims.correction: The official MIDA press release I accessed did not state the RM1.1 billion figure; the figure is supported by Reuters and MIDA's reposted news coverage.

claims.claim: Rafizi handed MACC 28 pages and a USB drive on 14 May 2026.
claims.source: Bernama, 14 May 2026; The Edge Malaysia, 14 May 2026
claims.correction: Bernama verifies the additional 28 pages. The Edge verifies printed documents and video recordings in a USB flash drive.

claims.claim: Rafizi submitted the evidence eight days after MACC recommended charges against two.
claims.source: Malay Mail, 6 May 2026 + Bernama, 14 May 2026
claims.correction: Arithmetic correct; retain 'recommended charges' not 'moved to charge'.

claims.claim: He was Economy Minister at the signing on 5 March 2025.
claims.source: PMO verbatim speech, 5 March 2025: https://www.pmo.gov.my/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-MARCH-2025-LAUNCHING-CEREMONY-STRATEGIC-PARTNERSHIP-IN-THE-SEMICONDUCTOR-INDUSTRY-1.pdf
claims.correction: PMO speech lists Rafizi as Economy Minister at the ceremony. It does not prove he personally signed the agreement.

claims.claim: The Arm deal was signed by Rafizi's ministry.
claims.source: MIDA, 5 March 2025; PMO verbatim speech, 5 March 2025
claims.correction: Say 'a government partnership involving the Ministry of Economy, MITI, MOF and MIDA' or 'MIDA and Arm announced/signed the agreement'. The PMO speech says the partnership was a cross-ministerial effort.

claims.claim: MACC has not named the two individuals it recommends for charges.
claims.source: Malay Mail + The Edge, 6 May 2026
claims.correction: No correction needed. Azam reported as saying he could not say who the two were.
claims.stale_check: No charges found in accessible search results up to 17 May 2026.

claims.claim: Akmal Nasrullah, the new Economy Minister, said on 23 February 2026 that the deal continues.
claims.source: Bernama, 23 Feb 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2526806; Reuters, 16 Dec 2025 (Akmal appointment)
claims.correction: No correction needed.

claims.claim: Akmal's reason was a 10-year structural commitment.
claims.source: Bernama 23 Feb 2026 + MIDA 5 Mar 2025 + Reuters 5 Mar 2025
claims.correction: Bernama supports back-end to front-end. Reuters supports payment over 10 years. MIDA's official release describes a FOUR-year strategic partnership. Separate 'payment/licence period 10 years' from 'official partnership 4 years'.

claims.claim: Abuse of power MACC Act s.23, cheating Penal Code s.420, CBT s.409.
claims.source: Malay Mail 6 May 2026 + MACC Act 2009 + Penal Code Act 574
claims.correction: Sections accurate. Caveat: these are reported as suggested investigation sections, not filed charges.

claims.claim: AGC holds the charging decision under Federal Constitution Article 145.
claims.source: Federal Constitution Article 145(3), AGC official text
claims.correction: Precisely: Article 145(3) gives AG discretion to institute, conduct or discontinue criminal proceedings, except Syariah/native/court-martial.

claims.claim: Azam Baki announced the recommendation 6 May 2026, six days before his tenure ended.
claims.source: The Edge 6 May 2026 + The Star 12 May 2026 (Azam clocks out) + The Edge 11 May 2026 (Azam tenure profile)
claims.correction: No correction needed, but avoid implying impropriety unless separately evidenced.

claims.claim: Rafizi resigned May 2025 after losing PKR deputy-president race to Nurul Izzah.
claims.source: Bernama 28 May 2025 + Rafizi LinkedIn statement 28 May 2025
claims.correction: No correction needed for resignation. 'Anwar's daughter' is factual but politically loaded; 'Nurul Izzah Anwar' is cleaner unless family relationship is analytically necessary.

claims.claim: Nurul Izzah beat Rafizi 9,803 to 3,866.
claims.source: Vote count appears in secondary sources (Wikipedia search snippet) but I did not find an official PKR result page.
claims.correction: Either cite an official PKR election result page or soften to 'lost the PKR deputy-presidency race to Nurul Izzah'. Do not use the exact vote count unless the primary result sheet is obtained.

claims.claim: Rafizi has been openly critical of the Madani government since resigning.
claims.source: Straits Times May 2026 + NST summary 2026
claims.correction: Broad characterization. Safer to cite one or two specific critical statements.

claims.claim: Nik Nazmi called the probe 'political conspiracy'.
claims.source: Reuters 16 Dec 2025 (Nik Nazmi resignation context); The Independent (Singapore) 6 May 2026: https://theindependent.sg/malaysia-risks-big-with-witch-hunt-of-ex-minister-pakatan-strongman/
claims.correction: The Independent reports Nik Nazmi asking whether the case is a bribery case or a political machination and frames it as a witch-hunt. Quote 'political machination' rather than 'political conspiracy' unless the original video/post confirms the exact word.

claims.claim: The question is which procurement track a 10-year, RM1.1B IP-licensing deal should have to clear.
claims.source: Bernama + Edge 14 May 2026; Reuters 5 Mar 2025
claims.correction: The procurement-track question is fair, but the description should disclose the 4-year-vs-10-year discrepancy and avoid implying the procurement track is already known.

claims.claim: The Government Procurement Act 2023 raises transparency standards for large-value contracts.
claims.source: Bernama 27 Aug 2025; MOF press 8 Sep 2025; Christopher & Lee Ong 19 Sep 2025
claims.correction: Replace 'Government Procurement Act 2023' with 'Government Procurement Bill 2025 / Government Procurement Act 2025, if enacted and in force'. It post-dated the 5 March 2025 Arm signing and so cannot be framed as a legal track the deal should have cleared at signing.
claims.stale_check: Not stale — the analysis uses the wrong year and likely wrong legal applicability.

claims.claim: NGO complaints alleged the agreement was rushed and skewed/one-sided.
claims.source: Bernama 14 May 2026 + The Edge 14 May 2026
claims.correction: Use 'rushed and skewed' depending on exact source wording. I did not find the NGOs' own complaint text or names.

claims.claim: Three local firms — SkyeChip, Oppstar, GreatAsic — received Arm IP/platform access.
claims.source: Ministry of Economy press statement, 11 May 2026: https://ekonomi.gov.my/sites/default/files/2026-05/Kenyataan%20Media%20YBM_Majlis%20Penyerahan%20Surat%20Tawaran%20Token%20Arm%20%5B11.05.26%5D_0.pdf; The Star 11 May 2026
claims.correction: No correction needed. Official Ministry statement verifies the three firms and approved tokens.

claims.claim: The deal keeps delivering chip-design IP access.
claims.source: Ministry of Economy press statement 11 May 2026 + Bernama 23 Feb 2026
claims.correction: 'Keeps delivering' is interpretive but supported by 11 May token handover after Akmal's 23 Feb continuity statement.

claims.claim: The MACC paper sits with the Attorney-General.
claims.source: The Edge 6 May 2026 + Malay Mail 6 May 2026
claims.correction: Available reporting says the investigation paper would be submitted to the DPP/AGC and the decision is not MACC's. I did not find an AGC public receipt confirmation.
claims.stale_check: Check AGC bulletins before publication for any post-6 May update.

claims.claim: The same coalition designed all three steps: deal continues, MACC recommendation, AGC decision.
claims.source: PMO Anwar sworn in 24 Nov 2022; Federal Constitution Article 145; MACC Act 2009
claims.correction: Fair to say the same PH-led unity government was in office throughout. Misleading to say the coalition 'designed' the MACC recommendation or AGC decision because those are institutional enforcement/prosecutorial functions, not coalition political acts.

claims.claim: Azam's 6 May announcement did not name the two and speculation should not be collapsed into fact.
claims.source: Malay Mail 6 May 2026 + Malaysiakini 6 May 2026
claims.correction: No correction needed — strong caution.
claims.stale_check: No charges found up to 17 May 2026.

claims.claim: Army-chief procurement probe and Arm Holdings probe are unrelated; RM11.4M/safehouse figures excluded.
claims.source: Reuters 29 Dec 2025 + Reuters 7 Jan 2026; Tom's Hardware 4 Mar 2026 (the conflating outlet)
claims.correction: Source note is useful. Analysis correctly excludes the conflation.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: MACC moved to charge two over RM1.1B Arm deal.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Accessible sources say MACC recommended or planned to recommend charges. MACC does not itself decide whether charges are filed; the DPP/AGC decides.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: Malay Mail 6 May 2026 + Federal Constitution Article 145

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: the RM1.1 billion Arm Holdings deal his ministry signed
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Official MIDA material says MIDA and ARM Limited announced or signed the partnership. PMO speech says it was cross-ministerial (Economy, MITI, MOF, MIDA). Does not prove Ministry of Economy was the signing party.

Reuters and MIDA news reposts support US$250M payment over 10 years; MIDA's official release describes a 4-year strategic partnership. Distinguish payment/licensing duration from official partnership duration.

Found references to a Government Procurement Bill 2025 passed in 2025, not a Government Procurement Act 2023. Since the Arm partnership was signed in March 2025, the later procurement legislation should not be framed as a legal track the deal had to clear at signing.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: MOF press citation 8 Sep 2025 + Bernama 27 Aug 2025

cardinal_sin_flags.why: Did not find an official PKR election result page in accessible search results. Vote count appears in secondary references; prompt requires primary verification for specific numbers.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: No official PKR primary source located.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: The same coalition designed all three steps.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Same PH-led unity government was in office, but MACC recommendation and AGC charging decision are institutional enforcement/prosecutorial processes. Saying 'designed' implies direct political authorship without evidence.

omitted_facts: MIDA's official 5 March 2025 release describes the MIDA-ARM collaboration as a four-year strategic partnership, while several news reports describe Malaysia paying US$250 million over ten years. The analysis should explicitly explain this duration discrepancy.
The Government Procurement Act reference appears wrong. Public sources point to a Government Procurement Bill 2025 debated and passed in 2025, not an Act 2023.

The official Ministry of Economy statement on 11 May 2026 confirms four tokens, including Arm CSS and Arm Flexible Access, were approved for Great Asic Technology Sdn Bhd, SkyeChip Bhd and Oppstar Technology Sdn Bhd.

Bernama reported MACC opened the investigation paper on 16 February 2026 after complaints by three NGOs alleging the agreement had been rushed and skewed, potentially exposing the government to financial risks.

I did not find the original NGO complaints, the names of the NGOs, an official MACC 6 May 2026 press release, an AGC receipt statement, or an e-Perolehan procurement record for the Arm arrangement in accessible search results.
source_assessment: The original analysis uses a reasonably broad set of media sources and includes important official anchors such as MIDA, Bernama, statutory text and PMO material. However, several core assertions still depend on secondary reporting rather than primary documents. The biggest gaps are the absence of the full MACC 6 May statement, any AGC statement, the procurement record or Cabinet minute, the NGO complaint text, the official PKR election result sheet for the vote count, and an Arm-side statement on the deal duration. Source diversity is moderate.
overall_assessment: The analysis is broadly reliable on the main event sequence. The major factual risk is the procurement-law framing: 'Government Procurement Act 2023' appears incorrect, and the analysis should not imply a 2025 agreement had to clear a later 2025 procurement statute. The second major risk is wording that overattributes the signing to Rafizi's ministry and compresses a four-year official partnership with a ten-year payment/licensing narrative. Publishable after tightening those points and removing unsupported exact vote-count and quotation details unless primary sources are added.
